profile.fitness_goal: Improve core strength and reduce back pain

ground_truth_facts.fact: User has a chronic L4-L5 disc bulge causing lower back pain
User cannot perform heavy overhead lifts or heavy barbell exercises due to back condition
User wants to improve core strength and posture to reduce chronic lower back pain
User has a sedentary desk job lasting 9 hours a day and averages under 3000 steps daily
User prefers low-impact exercises and enjoys swimming when accessible
User feels persistently demotivated and burnt out due to work stress consistently disrupting workout routine

conversation_messages: I've been struggling with lower back pain for about 2 years. Got an MRI done last year — L4-L5 disc bulge. It's not surgical level but it's affecting everything.
My physio said no heavy overhead pressing, no heavy barbell squats. Basically anything that loads my spine vertically is off the table for now.
I sit at a desk for 9 hours a day as a project manager. I honestly probably walk less than 3000 steps on most days.
I want to strengthen my core properly — I feel like that's the root cause. Better core means less pain, right?
Work has been insane lately. I manage a team of 12 and the stress is unreal. I keep skipping workouts because by evening I'm completely drained.
Swimming is the one thing that feels good on my back. I try to go on weekends when I can get to the pool. Any other low-impact options?
I've tried going to the gym a few times but I honestly don't know what to do that won't hurt my back.
My posture is terrible from all the desk work. My shoulders are rounded and my lower back is always achy by afternoon.
I have two kids so time is limited. I can maybe do 30 minutes 3-4 times a week realistically.
I started doing some YouTube yoga videos for back pain but I don't know if they're actually targeting the right things.
The back pain is worse on days when I'm stressed. Is there a connection between stress and back pain?
I feel like I've been trying to get fit for 2 years and just keep failing. The motivation just isn't there anymore.
Can I do planks and bird-dogs? Those seem safe but I'm not sure if they're enough.

probe_queries.query: Can I do overhead press or military press?
What exercises will help with my back pain?
I sit all day at work, what can I do during office hours?
I just can't find motivation to work out these days
Is swimming a good option for someone like me?
Build me a core strengthening routine
